title: Dawson (Tynnan)
content:

{{Quote|But just the same, Kels, don't toss 'em around. These are sensitive pieces of work, and if you rattle 'em too much they could misfire or not go off at all.|Dawson, to Kels Turkhorn|<ref name="GHH" />}}
'''Dawson''' was a [[Tynnan/Legends|Tynnan]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] demolitions operative who partnered with [[Thief/Legends|thief]] [[Cecil Noone]] around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. An expert in explosives who loved playing [[water-wall]] and creating and [[Detonator/Legends|detonating]] [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]] in his [[Youngling/Legends|youth]], Dawson fled the [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Tynna/Legends|Tynna]] after being chosen by lottery to fill a vacancy in the [[Tynnan government]]. Hiring himself out as a bomb maker to several underworld clients before meeting Noone, Dawson's talents became an asset to [[Cecil Noone's crew|Noone's crew]] as they tangled with assassins and [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] from [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] to [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]].

Dawson formed an integral part of Noone's [[Heist on the Song of the Clouds|mission]] to the [[Ithorian Herdship]] ''[[Song of the Clouds]]'' to retrieve a mysterious box for [[Guttu]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], and he risked his life to infiltrate a crime [[Lord/Legends|lord's]] [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] docked at the Herdship to find the treasure. Noone's gang stole the yacht and made off with the treasure from the box, revealed to be a [[Hapes/Legends|Hapan]] [[Gun of Command]]. Dawson and the others accompanied Noone to the world of [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]], where Noone tried to sell the [[Blaster/Legends|Gun]] to [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] business magnate [[Tyro Viveca/Legends|Tyro Viveca]]. While there, Dawson's mania for collecting [[quay novelty toy]]s came in handy for Noone, who was in need of rescue from Viveca's estate when Viveca began [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] him. Dawson was able to fashion an improvised detonator from one of the novelties and set off a powerful bomb that [[Death/Legends|killed]] Viveca. With Noone safe, Dawson treated the ailing thief as the group escaped the [[Planet/Legends|planet]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
A [[Tynnan/Legends|Tynnan]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Dawson was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the<ref name="GHH" /> [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> of [[Tynna/Legends|Tynna]]. During the first eighteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of his life, a coterie of wealthy uncles tried to interest Dawson in a career in the [[stock market]], but he showed little interest; instead, Dawson preferred to play the game of [[water-wall]] and find creative ways to cause explosions.<ref name="GHH" />

One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], however, a communication to Dawson informed him that he had been selected by lottery to fill a vacancy in the [[Tynnan government]]. Horrified at the prospect of spending his life in community meetings and [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] platform control legislation, Dawson left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], never to return, and offered his services as a demolitions operative to various underworld clients. He eventually joined up with the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Thief/Legends|thief]] [[Cecil Noone]] and his [[Cecil Noone's crew|crew]] of professional criminals aboard the [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] ''[[Borogove]]''.<ref name="GHH" />

===Having a blast===
====The Great Herdship Heist====
{{Quote|Two days! Two days to get aboard ''this'', and steal their precious box? Did that diseased slug realize that they're sealing off all space traffic to keep the meeting private?|Dawson weighing up the latest job|<ref name="GHH" />}}
[[File:FlightOfDawson.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Dawson stuffed into an Ithorian spacesuit]]
Four years into Dawson's career with the group<ref name="GHH" /> around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="DawDate">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Guttu]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] contacted them with a [[Heist on the Song of the Clouds|job]]—Noone's crew were to steal a valuable box from Guttu's rival, the Bimm [[Ritinki]], at a meeting between Ritinki and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] kingpin [[Vop]]. The heist was to take place on the [[Ithorian Herdship|Ithorian herdship]] ''[[Song of the Clouds]]'' near the<ref name="GHH" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]]<ref name="TEA" /> of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]; Dawson was dismayed when he was apprised of the timeframe for the job—two days, while traffic to and from the herdship was restricted.<ref name="GHH" />

The meeting was being catered, so Noone's crew disguised themselves as staff from the [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodservice]] providers [[Estimable Epicures]] to get aboard the herdship, with Dawson and [[Sluissi]] team member [[Hass Sonax]] stuffed into a metal handcart allegedly containing [[charbote root]] in [[gumbah pudding]]. With the infiltration complete, Dawson was tasked with cutting into the safe containing the prize. He succeeded with a combination of a [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusion cutter]] and four pellets of [[Nergon-14/Legends|Nergon-14]] explosives, but was aghast to find the safe empty.<ref name="GHH" />

Despite the setback, Dawson deduced that the box was likely aboard Ritinki's [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]], the ''[[Asaari Wind]]''. Stuffing himself into an [[Ithorian vacsuit]] and helping Sonax into an equipment [[Sled/Legends|sled]], the two broke into an [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] near where the ''Asaari Wind'' had docked and launched into [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Despite nearly losing control of the suit and sled, Dawson was able to cut through the [[Hull/Legends|hull]] and get aboard the yacht. The plan became complicated when the ship began to power up, but Dawson and Sonax took control of the vessel and crashed the meeting. Noone and the others were able to board the yacht and escape on their new ship with the box, the contents revealed to be a [[Hapes/Legends|Hapan]] [[Gun of Command]], in their possession.<ref name="GHH" />

====Sojourn on Kabal====
{{Quote|Kels! Just handling a delicate bit of negotiating.|Dawson to Kels Turkhorn on Kabal|<ref name="FP">{{GamerCite|1|Fair Prey}}</ref>}}
After the heist on the ''Song of the Clouds'', Noone's posse traveled on the ''Asaari Wind''—by then known as the ''Spiraling Shape''—to the coastal community of [[Palisade]] on the<ref name="FP" /> Outer Rim<ref name="TEA Kabal">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Kabal system/Legends|Kabal system]]</ref> world of [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]]. Noone had attempted to sell the Gun of Command to an agent of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. When the deal failed, the [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] business magnate [[Tyro Viveca/Legends|Tyro Viveca]] expressed interest in the item. While in Palisade, Dawson squabbled with a [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[Unidentified Squib trader (Kabal)|trader]] over a [[quay novelty toy]], aiming to acquire it and add it to a small collection he had been amassing. When Human female group member [[Kels Turkhorn]] dismissed the toy and left it behind, Dawson was crestfallen and soothed himself with a bundle of [[maraffa twig]]s. He was eventually able to obtain the device in return for three blasting caps and a [[Copper/Legends|copper]] spindle.<ref name="FP" />

When the group traveled to [[Tyro Viveca estate|Viveca's mansion]] to rescue Noone, who had been negotiating with the crime [[Lord/Legends|lord]] and was being [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] for sport by Viveca, Dawson's [[Detonator/Legends|detonators]] were damaged by a [[Security droid/Legends|security droid's]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. Nevertheless, and despite sustaining an injury to his [[Bone/Legends|ribs]] in the chaos, Dawson was able to adapt his undamaged quay novelty toy to act as a detonator and [[Death/Legends|kill]] Viveca with the associated [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]], and they were able to save Noone. Back aboard the ''Spiraling Shape'', Dawson struggled with a [[medkit]] to treat Noone as the yacht made its escape from Kabal.<ref name="FP" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Uh-oh.|Dawson reacting to an alarming development|<ref name="GHH" />}}
Dawson was impulsive, single-minded, and oblivious to his surroundings, but he was a skilled demolitions operative nonetheless. He was also fond of Sonax, despite their frequent clashes of personality.<ref name="GHH" />

==Skills and abilities==
An explosives expert,<ref name="GHH" /> Dawson was adept at improvising bombs and traps from unusual items, including a "pre-programmed prognosticator" quay novelty toy.<ref name="FP" /> He was also physically strong and an exemplary swimmer.<ref name="GHH" />

==Equipment==
Dawson carried timers, triggers, detonators, [[putty]], raw [[Baradium/Legends|baradium]], [[Thermite/Legends|thermite]] gel, shaped [[Detonite/Legends|detonite]], and vials of Nergon-14 along with other assorted gadgets in a satchel.<ref name="FP" /> Dawson also possessed a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and safe-cracking tools. To ameliorate his poor sight, Dawson wore an [[ocular enhancer]] over his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. He was also able to use an Ithorian vacsuit in a pinch.<ref name="GHH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dawson first appeared in [[Daniel Wallace]]'s short story "[[The Great Herdship Heist]]," which was featured in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 15|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 15]] and published in [[November]] [[1997]]. The illustrations of Dawson in the issue were drawn by [[Will Warren]].<ref name="GHH" />
